mechanism: DPP-4 inhibition preserves incretin hormones (GLP-1, GIP)

selectivity_discovery.key_finding: GLU205 in DPP-4 vs LYS205 in DPP-8 vs ARG205 in DPP-9
selectivity_discovery.strategy: Positive peptide charges bind DPP-4 GLU, repelled by DPP-8/9 LYS/ARG

selectivity_anchors.strategy: Add POSITIVE charge (Arg/Lys) to peptide - will bind DPP-4 GLU205 but be REPELLED by DPP-8 LYS / DPP-9 ARG
Add POSITIVE charge - salt bridge with DPP-4 GLU206, no interaction with DPP-8/9 neutral residues
Add NEGATIVE charge - binds DPP-4 ARG358, repelled by hydrophobic DPP-8/9
Add NEGATIVE charge (Asp/Glu) - salt bridge with DPP-4 ARG125

peptide_designs.name: DPP4_Z2_SEL_RK
peptide_designs.selectivity: GLU205 in DPP-4 vs LYS205 in DPP-8: positive peptide charges BIND DPP-4 but are REPELLED by DPP-8

peptide_designs.name: DPP4_Z2_SEL_DUAL
peptide_designs.selectivity: Engage both GLU (205/206) and ARG (125/358) unique to DPP-4

peptide_designs.name: DPP4_Z2_ARO_001
peptide_designs.selectivity: Dense aromatic network matches DPP-4 S1 pocket + charge selectivity

peptide_designs.name: DPP4_Z2_MIN_001
peptide_designs.selectivity: Minimal design focusing on critical selectivity residues

peptide_designs.name: DPP4_Z2_GLP1_001
peptide_designs.selectivity: GLP-1 scaffold with selectivity residues inserted

best_design.name: DPP4_Z2_SEL_RK
best_design.rationale: R1K4 for GLU205/206 selectivity + W2W5 for Z² stacking
